pattern: A single pass with a running set lets you ask, for each value, whether its half or its double has already appeared, turning a quadratic pair search into linear work.
hints: For a fixed value, only two other values could pair with it: the one twice as big and the one half as big. You do not need to compare every pair.
Remember the values you have already walked past in a set, then for the current value check membership of its double and (if it is even) its half.
Treat zero carefully: zero is its own double, so it only counts when zero appears at least twice. Track seen values and test 2*v and v/2 against that set before inserting v.

edge_inputs.description: the double comes before the original in the array
a single zero is not its own double, so this is false
two zeros do satisfy the doubling relation
an odd value has no integer half present

stress.description: moderate array over a small value range so doubling pairs and zero-pairs both arise
stress.note: narrow range maximises collisions and zero handling
